prompt: A new request arrived and the scope is unclear. Choose the track, active hub, next skill, and update workflow-state.
One active request already has routing and state. Drive the next solid handoff without reopening planning.
This work is larger than quick-flow and planning artifacts are stale. Use the planning hub to sequence analyst, PM, architect, and story slicing without losing context.
Use developer because the approved story is implementation-ready. Read the story, edit code in one controlled slice, run the execution loop, collect test evidence, and update workflow-state.
Use test-hub after implementation exists and confidence is lower than the change impact. Build the evidence matrix, coordinate verification, collect regression evidence, and prepare qa-report inputs.
Work is about to be called done. Check acceptance criteria, risk, regression scope, and write a QA report.
Before deploy, produce a readiness verdict with smoke signals, rollback guardrails, and release evidence.
A naming cutover might leave stale compatibility tokens behind. Scan source and runtime files with a strict fail-closed naming gate.
The chat is getting long and may resume in a new thread. Create checkpoint, rehydrate context, handoff, and diff continuity.
The agent is about to say fixed and ready. Run one last evidence pass before making the completion claim.
List completion claims and map each claim to fresh proof output without deciding overall readiness.
Implementation evidence exists. Produce a formal go or no-go readiness verdict against acceptance criteria, risk, regression scope, and qa-report.
Use api-integration for network-facing code. We are changing API clients, webhooks, auth, retries, timeouts, and error mapping for an external service.
Use data-persistence before touching schemas, repositories, migrations, transactions, caches, and consistency boundaries.
Use dependency-management because a package is being added or upgraded and lockfiles, version pinning, dependency risks, toolchain, and CI drift must be checked.

Use multimodal-evidence for a static screenshot artifact. Inspect the image, describe visible observations, confidence, affected acceptance criteria, and follow-up checks without a live browser session.
Use browser-inspector because the active hub needs live browser state from a repro route. Collect console, network, DOM, layout, performance facts, reproduction confidence, and captured artifacts.
Use media-tooling when screenshots, assets, or content files need transformations, reversible processing, exact asset sources, outputs, and evidence packaging for the current lane.
Use api-integration for payment webhook clients. Document auth, retries, timeouts, idempotency, request and response patterns, and error mapping for the external service.
Use data-persistence for order ledger schemas, repositories, migrations, transactions, caches, consistency boundaries, and rollback notes.
Use dependency-management before upgrading a browser driver package. Check package manager, lockfiles, version pinning, dependency risks, toolchain setup, CI drift, and transitive risk.
Use browser-inspector on a live checkout route. Collect console errors, network requests, DOM state, performance facts, reproduction confidence, and captured artifacts.
Use multimodal-evidence for before and after static screenshot artifacts. Report visible observations, confidence, affected acceptance criteria, and follow-up checks without a live browser session.
Use media-tooling for content file transformations. Normalize image assets, preserve exact asset sources, write deterministic outputs, and package evidence for the active lane.
Use accessibility-review before completion claims for frontend work. Check keyboard navigation, visible focus, semantic structure, labels, contrast, and hold verdict evidence.
Use policy-guard for high-risk agent operations. Scan shell commands, path traversal, hard-coded secrets, prompt-injection phrases, and enforce fail-closed handling.
Use impact-radar before touching runtime, adapters, templates, scripts, docs, and packaging so blast radius and verification gates are explicit.

Need project-architecture support, not architect planning. Analyze existing codebase shape, entry points, execution flow, dependency direction, architecture drift, hotspots, and update the living architecture reference.
Use ux-structure when a user-facing flow needs sharper information hierarchy, cleaner interaction flow, loading empty error states, and less generic AI-looking UI.

Use bootstrap when starting a repo lane and workflow-state or project-context is missing or stale. Initialize or refresh the shared Relay-kit runtime, record artifacts, and create team-board if the request may split into lanes.

Use debug-hub because work starts from a regression, flaky behavior, or unexplained mismatch. Reproduce the issue, collect logs and test output, write investigation-notes, and decide whether the next move is fix-hub, test-hub, or plan-hub.

Use fix-hub after debug-hub validated findings. Convert the known problem into a bounded implementation path, update tech-spec with files and verification steps, name what must not change, and hand off to developer.

Use review-hub when requirements, architecture, implementation, and quality evidence may disagree. Check alignment, decide go forward or bounce to planning, debugging, implementation, or hold for missing evidence.
Use pm after discovery when the work needs buildable scope. Translate product brief or scoped request into PRD, release slices, acceptance criteria, risks, mitigations, and epics.

Use architect when PRD exists or a change alters module boundaries, data flow, security, or operations. Convert requirements into implementation-ready architecture with trade-offs and a readiness verdict.

Use scrum-master when planning is done and work must be sliced into safe verifiable increments. Turn PRD, architecture, epics, or tech-spec into story files with acceptance criteria and verification steps.

Use runtime-doctor when runtime integrity may have drifted. Run deterministic diagnostics for adapter parity, generated artifacts, live state-mode checks, missing skills, extra skills, and placeholder state markers.
Use analyst before PRD or architecture. Clarify product intent, assumptions, target users, open questions, constraints, and produce a product brief for an unclear opportunity.
Use brainstorm-hub because the request is still a rough idea and opportunity. Guide early ideation, explore directions, and frame the problem before formal planning.
Use execution-loop while building a code fix iteratively. Run a self-correcting development loop, collect test evidence, and do not claim completion until verification passes.
Use scout-hub when the repo area is unfamiliar or stale. Reconnoiter code entrypoints, likely drift, dependencies, and living references before planning or debugging continues.
Use team when work must proceed in parallel across multiple lanes. Coordinate owners, lane locks, handoffs, and avoid agents stepping on each other.
Use testing-patterns when adding tests or fixtures. Capture test strategy, mocks, dependency boundaries, regression coverage, and what evidence is enough for release confidence.
Use doc-pointers because the hub needs exact docs fragments, file paths, and source references before deciding the next implementation move.
Use handoff-context because the next skill needs a tighter relevant context handoff, focused context pack, and include/exclude list before it can act safely.
Use memory-search because the hub needs past decisions, handoff breadcrumbs, and prior debug evidence from .relay-kit artifacts before planning continues.
Use mermaid-diagrams because the architecture sequence needs a quick Mermaid diagram, flow, and sequencing representation inside the artifact.
Use problem-solving because the hub needs hypotheses, trade-offs, and resolution paths grounded in current evidence before choosing a fix direction.
Use repo-map because the hub needs a quick dependency map, file tree slice, and entrypoint overview before touching this unfamiliar module.
Use research because the hub needs fresh evidence for a technical decision while retaining ownership of the lane and marking assumptions.
Use root-cause-debugging because the hub needs disciplined investigation, disproven hypotheses, and root-cause notes before proposing fixes.
Use sequential-thinking because the hub needs structured thought, stepwise reasoning, explicit steps, and checkpoints without changing ownership.
Use skill-evolution when upgrading a Relay-kit SKILL.md. Audit trigger descriptions, allowed tools, handoff contract, and scenario fixtures before changing behavior.
Use skill-gauntlet when runtime skill behavior may have drifted and a regression gate must validate routing and completion claims before trusting the runtime.
Use test-first-development because implementation should follow a red-green-refactor loop: write the failing test first, make it green, then clean up one behavior.
Use context-continuity when a long Relay-kit lane needs source freshness, checkpoint, rehydrate, handoff, and diff continuity before dashboard evidence is trusted.
Use team when multi-lane state needs lane locks, depends_on, wave_id, resume_condition, merge order, and a relay-kit lane audit before parallel work continues.
Use runtime-doctor when adapter diagnostics must check adapter parity, missing skills, extra skills, metadata drift, and live mode runtime integrity before release.
Use memory-search when the hub needs ranked state and contract lookup with file paths, line-level excerpts, stale hits, and prior decisions for dashboard evidence.
Use repo-map when service-boundary mapping needs entrypoints, modules, dependency direction, and impact surface for CLI, registry, gates, support, release, publication, and Pulse.
Use go-service-engineering for a Go API service update with Echo or Gin routing, GORM or sqlc persistence, Redis cache behavior, job workers, middleware, and observability.
Use next-product-frontend for a Next.js App Router feature with server and client component boundaries, server actions, shadcn UI, Tailwind styling, cache behavior, and accessibility checks.
Use growth-marketing to produce positioning, landing page messaging, SEO intent, launch plan, funnel metrics, and campaign QA for a product release.
Use market-research for competitor scan, ICP validation, pricing signal analysis, source authority ranking, and decision-impact notes.
Use automation-ops for scheduler and webhook automation with dry-run support, retry policy, rollback runbook, failure handling, and operations ownership.
Use vietnamese-product-localization for Vietnamese and bilingual product docs, support communication, release notes, terminology consistency, and localization quality checks.
Use token-economy when context is too large and you must estimate tokens, classify raw-required versus compressible blocks, and report budget violations before implementation starts.
Use token-economy to build a task-scoped context pack with authority and freshness ranking, selected versus dropped sources, and max-tokens enforcement.
Use token-economy when test output includes traceback and assertion failures so strict mode keeps raw pointers and does not hide critical failure evidence.
A user runs /relay-token-audit and expects token budget, signal retention, raw-required blocks, and context pack safety checks in one report.
Use mmo-reup-automation to design a reup flow with attribution checks, deterministic dedupe keys, posting windows, and frequency controls before publish.
Use mmo-account-operations for multi-account lifecycle control with onboarding, active and suspended states, credential rotation, per-account action budgets, and escalation runbooks.
Use mmo-browser-fleet-automation when profile isolation, session lease controls, stable selectors, explicit waits, and retry backoff are required for browser fleet reliability.
Use mmo-social-marketing-automation for campaign scheduling through official APIs with quota budgets, duplicate-content guardrails, moderation checks, and policy-safe execution.
Use mmo-lowcode-automation for no-code and low-code workflows with trigger ownership, queue pressure controls, module-level retries, and publish-versus-draft safeguards.
Use mmo-mobile-app-automation for emulator and device matrix execution with app-state preconditions, selector strategy, wait policy, and failure triage evidence.
Use mmo-cloud-operations-automation to run scheduled jobs with queue boundaries, retry jitter, idempotency keys, duplicate prevention, and cost ceiling controls.
Use mmo-http-api-automation for contract-safe endpoint orchestration with auth scope checks, 429 handling, backoff, idempotent writes, and replay safety logs.

Use signal-calibration when an answer claims field-tested, production-ready, commercial-ready, or maximum skill quality and must map the claim to proof_level, confidence, evidence_sources, overclaim flags, residual risk, and next verification before acceptance.

Use frontend-design to build a production product surface with reference-driven hierarchy, visual density controls, loading empty error states, screenshot evidence, and anti-generic AI UI review before completion.

Use ui-styling for a responsive component system with design tokens, accessible dialog and form states, focus handling, contrast checks, and deliberate styling rather than default component-library cards.

Use aesthetic when a rendered dashboard looks obviously AI-generated. Compare screenshots against references, critique hierarchy, flag equal-card filler, and require a revision loop before visual quality claims.

Use mmo-identity-infrastructure for authorized multi-account operations that need profile inventory, proxy-to-profile binding, locale consistency, versioned identity specs, and policy-safe operator evidence.
Use mmo-proxy-network-ops for proxy pool segmentation, health monitoring, sticky session assignment, blacklist checks, cost ceiling, and an audited assignment registry for account operations.
Use mmo-nick-warmup-engine for account warmup with phase gates, action budgets, trust-signal checkpoints, cooldown recovery, and readiness verdict before higher-risk account workflows.
Use mmo-ecommerce-multichannel for marketplace automation with master SKU registry, inventory sync, buffer stock, order routing, logistics labels, and oversell prevention evidence.
Use mmo-content-factory for AI-assisted bulk content production with variant diversity scores, platform-specific formats, scheduling windows, human review gates, and performance feedback loops.
Use mmo-crypto-wallet-farming for crypto farming operations with wallet isolation, capital risk budget, on-chain behavior variance, security controls, gas cost planning, and Sybil-risk evidence.
Use mmo-data-harvesting for authorized data collection with source authorization, deduplication, encrypted PII handling, segmentation schema, seeding target lists, and human review gates.
